patient_profile.name: Monique Harrison
patient_profile.lifestyle_summary: Monique is a middle-income student living in a suburban area who stays moderately active and has a high-salt diet. She is a former smoker but consumes alcohol heavily.
patient_profile.past_medical_history: Monique has a history of hypertension and experienced a major depressive episode in her mid-30s. She has no known psychosis, seizures, or hospitalizations for mental health crises.
patient_profile.family_history: Her mother had severe depression and her younger brother was diagnosed with Bipolar Disorder Type I. No significant history of mental health conditions in other relatives.
patient_profile.medication_history: She previously took sertraline for depression but discontinued it due to feelings of excessive activation. Currently not on psychiatric medication.
patient_profile.allergy_history: No known drug or environmental allergies.
patient_profile.vaccination_history: Routine vaccinations up-to-date, including influenza and COVID-19 vaccines.

symptom_trajectory.chief_complaint: I’ve been feeling a mix of really high energy and really low moments, and it’s getting harder to keep up with my schoolwork and life.
symptom_trajectory.onset: Symptoms gradually worsened over the past 2 months.
symptom_trajectory.duration: Current episode has lasted approximately 8 weeks with intermittent fluctuations.
symptom_trajectory.progression: Initially noticed mild restlessness and occasional low mood, which evolved into distinct manic and depressive states lasting several days each.

selected_symptoms.name: elevated or extremely irritable mood
selected_symptoms.impact: Caused social tensions and difficulty focusing on academic tasks.

selected_symptoms.name: racing thoughts
selected_symptoms.duration: Intermittent during manic phases, lasting hours
selected_symptoms.impact: Interfered with her ability to plan and prioritize schoolwork effectively.

selected_symptoms.name: low or anxious mood
selected_symptoms.impact: Caused feelings of hopelessness and neglect of personal hygiene.

selected_symptoms.name: sleep disturbance
selected_symptoms.impact: Caught between insomnia during manic phases and oversleeping during depressive phases leading to exhaustion.

selected_symptoms.name: impaired responsibilities or relationships
selected_symptoms.impact: Struggled to maintain academic deadlines and interpersonal engagements.

negative_symptoms: psychotic symptoms like hallucinations or delusions
symptom_trajectory.functional_impact: Her ability to balance academic and personal responsibilities is moderately impaired, but she can perform some tasks intermittently. Social interactions are strained.
risk_features: No urgent risk features such as suicidal ideation or psychosis observed at this time.
